name: chile colorado
instructions: rehydrate the guajillo and california chiles. saute yellow onions and garlic, then blend the sauteed onions, garlic, and rehydrated chiles together into a smooth paste. fry the paste in a pan, then simmer with the meat (or beans) in bouillon. for chili powder, new mexico chili powder is best — NOT paprika/cumin blends. add masa harina near the end to thicken the sauce. liquid largely cooks off; the goal is a thick red sauce coating meat or beans, not a soupy stew.
notes:
INTRO: this is not traditional chile colorado — traditional chile colorado uses real beef (often chuck) slow-cooked for hours. we use ground beef or vegan ground beef and skip the long braise.

BLENDED ONIONS: blending the onions smooth with the chiles is important. if you leave the onions diced (and especially if you add tomato), you end up with something closer to american chili — a different recipe entirely (diced peppers, onions, tomato, simmered as a stew). chile colorado is about a smooth chile-onion-garlic paste fried down into a thick sauce.

CHILES: california and guajillo only. ancho and pasilla are traditional in many chile colorado recipes but i don't like them as much here — they pull the sauce darker and more bitter than i want.

CHICKEN: chicken is not good in the final dish (which should be composed of only solid beans or dark/red meat). but chicken thighs can be cooked in the broth and then REMOVED to make a chicken side dish coated in the red sauce, while also flavoring the rest of the chili. don't leave the chicken in the final pot.

BEANS: pinto is the canonical bean. white beans are fine alongside pinto, but never use white beans instead of pinto — pinto carries the dish.

NO TOMATO: tomato in any form (fresh, canned, paste) is incorrect for chile colorado. that's american chili.
